objective: Prepare Spring Boot backend testing with JUnit 5, Mockito, AssertJ, MockMvc, architecture/component/contract/integration/unit tests, JaCoCo coverage at 99% or higher, backend load-test parity with the prior C# implementation, and Java/JVM diagnostic compatibility.
changed: Expanded backend test suites across unit, web, component, architecture, contract, and integration layers.
Added Maven JaCoCo coverage-check profile for 99% instruction and line coverage.
Updated backend coverage script to enforce thresholds and report executed test counts.
Updated backend tagged-suite scripts to fail on zero executed tests.
Added backend coverage jobs to dev and main runtime CI workflows.
Added tests/contracts/pacts to the main runtime promotion allowlist for promoted coverage and contract jobs.
Added Spring Boot real backend load-test endpoints for start/status/metrics/stop.
Added k6 execution, fake deterministic runs, active-run control, per-run report artifacts, and metrics parsing.
Added JVM runtime diagnostics while preserving frontend-compatible legacy process/gc/threadPool/database fields.
Updated load-test configuration for base-url, report-root, and k6-bin.
Updated frontend load-test dashboard visible wording to backend runtime and JDBC terminology.
Fixed ContentService context null PublishedAt handling exposed by tests.
intentionallyNotChanged: Branch coverage is measured but not gated.
No separate staging branch workflow was added.
Main is promoted through the existing promotion PR flow rather than pushed directly.
No unrelated frontend behavior was changed; frontend edits are limited to load-test dashboard diagnostic compatibility and Java-neutral visible wording.
validations: bash -n backend test and coverage scripts
Docker Java 21 full backend coverage gate against fresh PostgreSQL
CI Dev run 25989003521 succeeded with backend coverage
CI Dev run 25989376008 succeeded after Pact allowlist fix
risks: Branch coverage remains measured but not part of the 99% gate.
Automatic promotion fails before checkout when PROMOTION_TOKEN is absent; manual release/main-promote PR promotion is being used.
After the load-test parity commit, dev CI, staging image publish, promotion PR CI, main merge, and main publish gates still require observation.
k6 must be installed in the runtime environment for real backend load-test runs; the fake runner is retained for deterministic tests.
The external coverage database container remains running for local evidence inspection.
recommendation: Commit and push the load-test parity slice to dev, verify CI Dev and Publish GHCR Dev, regenerate release/main-promote, verify CI Main Runtime, merge to main, and observe main push/publish gates.
